title: Methods and composition for preparing viscous substrate
abstract: Methods and composition of preparation of viscous substrate for use in enzyme immunoassays has been described. In particular, the viscous enzyme substrate contains one or more viscosity increasing agents such as carboxy methyl cellulose and one or more colorimetric/ chromogenic substrate such as TMB (3,3',5,5'-tetramethylbenzidine) or OPD (o-phenylenediamine dihydrochloride) or ABTS (2,2'-Azinobis [3-ethylbenzothiazoline-6-sulfonic acid]-diammonium slat) for use with HRP (horse radish peroxidase) or pNPP (p-nitrophenyl phosphate, disodium slat) for use with AP (Alkaline Phosphatase). The invention further discloses the use of viscous enzyme substrate for localized color development which is visible to the naked eye.
claims:
Claims Claims im: 1. A method for preparation of Viscous Substrate, comprising: a chromogenic agent consisting of (TMB [3,3â,5,5â-tetramethylbenzidine] or OPD [o-phenyl enedi am i ne di hyd roch loride] or ABTS [2 , 2â-Azi nobis [3- ethylbenzothiazoline-6-sulfonic acid]-diammonium slat] for use with HRP (horse radish peroxidase) or pNPP [p-nitrophenyl phosphate, disodium slat] along with AP (Alkaline Phosphatase); a substrate comprising of a chromogenic agent, a viscosity agent, a Hydrogen peroxide, a DMSO and a glycerol for use in enzyme immunoassays to generate a localized color. 2. The method according to claim 1 wherein, the viscosity agent is selected from the following classes (a) natural gums such as acacia, tragacanth, algninic acid and guar gum etc., (b) cellulose derivatives such as carboxy methyl cellulose (CMC), methyl cellulose, ethyl cellulose, hydroxyl ethyl cellulose, hydroxyl propyl methyl cellulose; (c) microcrystalline cellulose; (d) chitosan; (e) synthetic polymers such as carbomer (polyacrylic acid), polyvinyl pyrrolidone, polyvinyl alcohol. 3. The method according to claim 1 , wherein, sodium thiosulfate is used a stabilizing agent. 4. The method according to claim 1 wherein, the enzyme immunoassay includes reaction between the enzyme and the substrate. 5. The method according to claim 1 wherein, the substrate is used as or single component or two components. 6. The method according to claim 1 wherein, the viscous substrate is used for analysis of biological samples such as protein, nucleic acid, bacteria, virus and carbohydrates. 7. The method according to claim 1 wherein, the prepared viscous substrate is used in rapid test kits across technologies such as micro-fluidic devices. 8. A composition for preparation of a viscous substrate, comprising: a chromogenic agent consisting of (TMB [3,3â,5,5â-tetramethylbenzidine] or OPD [o-phenyl enedi am i ne di hyd roch loride] or ABTS [2 , 2â-Azi nobis [3- ethylbenzothiazoline-6-sulfonic acid]-diammonium slat] for use with HRP (horse radish peroxidase) or pNPP [p-nitrophenyl phosphate, disodium slat] along with AP (Alkaline Phosphatase); a substrate comprising of a chromogenic agent, a viscosity agent, a Hydrogen peroxide, a DMSO and a glycerol for use in enzyme immunoassays to generate a localized color. 9. The composition according to claim 8 wherein, the viscosity agent is selected from the following classes (a) natural gums such as acacia, tragacanth, algninic acid and guar gum etc., (b) cellulose derivatives such as carboxy methyl 

 cellulose (CMC), methyl cellulose, ethyl cellulose, hydroxyl ethyl cellulose, hydroxyl propyl methyl cellulose; (c) microcrystalline cellulose; (d) chitosan; (e) synthetic polymers such as carbomer (polyacrylic acid), polyvinyl pyrrolidone, polyvinyl alcohol. 10. The composition according to claim 9, wherein, the CMC is used at a concentration of 0.001 % to 2%. 11. The composition according to claim 8 wherein, the H2O2 is used at a concentration of 0.001 to 2%. 12. The composition according to claim 8 wherein, the DMSO is used at a concentration of 0.001 -60%. 13. The composition according to claim 8 wherein, the glycerol is used at a concentration of 0.001 to 50%.
